0.original: He'll drink a couple of extra cups of coffee, maybe cruise around the neighborhood a while, 2 00:00:01,615 --> 00:00:05,505 or do a dozen little things to postpone that first call. 3 00:00:06,175 --> 00:00:08,544 I train each new man this way. 4 00:00:08,655 --> 00:00:12,465 I explain to him that the only way to start is to start. 5 00:00:12,574 --> 00:00:13,905 Don't deliberate. 6 00:00:14,015 --> 00:00:16,064 Don't postpone getting started. 7 00:00:16,175 --> 00:00:16,945 Do this. 8 00:00:17,054 --> 00:00:22,225 Just park your car, get your sample case, walk to the door, ring the bell, 9 00:00:22,495 --> 00:00:29,344 smile, say good morning, and make your presentation, all mechanically, without a lot of conscious thought. 10 00:00:29,775 --> 00:00:32,945 Start making calls this way and you break the ice. 11 00:00:33,135 --> 00:00:38,785 By the second or third call, your mind is sharp and your presentations become effective. 12 00:00:39,294 --> 00:00:45,905 A humorist once said the most difficult problem in life was getting out of a warm bed into a cold room, 13 00:00:46,015 --> 00:00:47,264 and he had a point. 14 00:00:47,535 --> 00:00:53,905 The longer you lie there and think how unpleasant it will be to get up, the more difficult it becomes. 15 00:00:54,175 --> 00:01:01,745 Even in such a simple operation as this, mechanical action, just throwing off the covers and putting your feet on the floor, 16 00:01:01,855 --> 00:01:03,265 defeats dread. 17 00:01:03,935 --> 00:01:05,344 The point is clear. 18 00:01:05,615 --> 00:01:10,544 People who get things done in this world don't wait for the spirit to move them. 19 00:01:10,735 --> 00:01:12,465 They move the spirit. 20 00:01:12,975 --> 00:01:14,864 Try these two exercises. 21 00:01:14,864 --> 00:01:16,625 1. 22 00:01:17,135 --> 00:01:23,985 Use the mechanical way to accomplish simple but sometimes unpleasant business and household chores. 23 00:01:24,255 --> 00:01:31,584 Rather than think about the unpleasant features of the task, jump right in and get going without a lot of deliberation. 24 00:01:33,294 --> 00:01:38,705 Perhaps the most unpleasant household task to most women is washing dishes. 25 00:01:38,895 --> 00:01:40,785 My mother is no exception. 26 00:01:41,054 --> 00:01:46,145 But she has mastered a mechanical approach to dispensing with this task quickly, 27 00:01:46,175 --> 00:01:48,945 so she can return to things she likes to do. 28 00:01:49,375 --> 00:01:56,705 As she leaves the table, she always mechanically picks up several dishes, and without thinking about the task ahead, 29 00:01:56,895 --> 00:01:58,225 just gets started. 30 00:01:58,415 --> 00:02:00,705 In just a few minutes, she is through. 31 00:02:00,975 --> 00:02:05,745 Doesn't this beat stacking dishes and dreading the unpleasant inevitable? 32 00:02:06,255 --> 00:02:07,584 Do this today. 33 00:02:07,775 --> 00:02:10,544 Pick the one thing you like to do least. 34 00:02:10,655 --> 00:02:15,665 Then, without letting yourself deliberate on or dread the task, do it. 35 00:02:15,935 --> 00:02:19,025 That's the most efficient way to handle chores. 36 00:02:22,015 --> 00:02:28,465 Next, use the mechanical way to create ideas, map out plans, solve problems,
0.b1: He will drink extra coffee, drive around the neighborhood, or do small things to delay his first call.
0.cn: 他会多喝几杯咖啡，在附近转转，或者做些琐事来推迟打第一个电话。
0.ai_notes:
'cruise around': to drive or travel slowly through an area without a specific destination.
'postpone': to delay something or put it off until a later time.
'the only way to start is to start': a common saying meaning that the hardest part of any task is beginning, so you should just take the first step.
'deliberate': to think about something very carefully and for a long time before making a decision.
'sample case': a bag or container used by salespeople to carry examples of the products they are selling.
'break the ice': to do or say something to make people feel more relaxed and comfortable in a new situation.
'he had a point': an expression used to agree with someone's opinion or argument.
'defeats dread': to overcome the feeling of fear or anxiety about doing something unpleasant.
'spirit to move them': an idiom meaning waiting for a feeling of inspiration or motivation to arrive before taking action.
'jump right in': to start doing something immediately and with energy.
'dispensing with': to get rid of or finish a task quickly.
'through': in this context, it means finished or completed with a task.
'unpleasant inevitable': a situation that is not enjoyable but must be done regardless.
'map out': to plan the details of something carefully.
0.remarks:
'cruise around': 在一个区域内缓慢行驶或游荡，没有明确的目的地。
'postpone': 推迟，延期。
'the only way to start is to start': 一句常用的谚语，意为任何任务最难的部分就是开始，所以你应该直接迈出第一步。
'deliberate': 在做决定前非常仔细且长时间地思考。
'sample case': 推销员用来携带产品样品的包或容器。
'break the ice': 在新环境中做或说些什么，使人们感到更放松、更自在。
'he had a point': 用于同意某人的观点或论点的表达方式。
'defeats dread': 克服对做某件不愉快事情的恐惧或焦虑感。
'spirit to move them': 一个习语，意为在采取行动之前等待灵感或动力出现。
'jump right in': 立即且充满活力地开始做某事。
'dispensing with': 摆脱或快速完成某项任务。
'through': 在此语境下，意为完成或结束某项任务。
'unpleasant inevitable': 一种虽然不愉快但必须去做的必然情况。
'map out': 仔细规划某事的细节。
